summary: Well-structured shared-meta blueprint cleanly mapping technology choices to architectural surfaces. Fully self-describing and scannable. The prescribes block wastes some density repeating "canonical" and only three when/then governance rules cover five declared surfaces.
strengths: Clean surface/technology separation makes the full stack recoverable at a glance
Defines block explicitly grounds 'canonical', 'TailwindCss', 'AgentBrowser', and 'stack exception'—low interpretive slack on key terms
Governance rules for new-framework introduction, styling divergence, and verification drift capture the highest-value drift scenarios
Fully self-contained: a reader recovers the entire canonical stack from this file plus the language root alone
weaknesses: Only three when/then rules despite five surfaces—runtime, backend, and build-tool bypass have no governance signal
The prescribes block repeats 'canonical' nine times; a shared preamble or grouped statement would be denser without losing clarity
Naming inconsistency: 'agent-browser' in prescribes text vs 'AgentBrowser' in Stack constant
No versioning or timestamp signal for when stack choices were adopted, limiting future staleness detection
